title: Commercial Bank Examination Manual, 3000—Capital, Earnings, Liquidity, and Sensitivity to Market Risk
summary: This Federal Reserve Commercial Bank Examination Manual section (3000) provides supervisory guidance on assessing capital adequacy for state member banks. Effective November 2020, it outlines the purpose of bank capital, the framework of Regulation Q (12 CFR Part 217), and the three-tier capital structure (CET1, Additional Tier 1, Tier 2). The document does not address macro catalysts or market structure topics relevant to trading or investment decisions.
key_points: Regulation Q (12 CFR Part 217) establishes risk-based capital requirements adopted in 2013 by the Federal Reserve, FDIC, and OCC to address weaknesses from the 2008-09 financial crisis
The three components of regulatory capital are: (1) Common Equity Tier 1 capital, (2) Additional Tier 1 capital, and (3) Tier 2 capital
Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) is the highest-quality capital, primarily composed of common stock and retained earnings, designed to absorb losses as they occur
Regulation Q applies to state member banks, US-domiciled bank holding companies, and covered savings and loan holding companies on a consolidated basis
Capital adequacy triggers the most supervisory action under the Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) provisions of section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
The risk-based capital ratio is calculated by dividing qualifying capital (numerator) by risk-weighted assets (denominator)
why_it_matters: This is internal supervisory guidance for bank examiners, not a market-moving document. It defines how regulators assess bank capital adequacy but contains no economic forecasts, policy signals, or market-relevant catalysts.

human_review.notes: This source does not qualify for the macro catalysts or market structure topics. It is an internal bank examination manual describing supervisory capital assessment frameworks. Recommend exclusion from archive or reclassification if bank capital regulations become a market-moving topic.

notes: NOT RELEVANT TO MACRO CATALYSTS OR MARKET STRUCTURE. It contains no market-moving information such as economic data, policy rate decisions, speeches, or market structure observations. The document describes Regulation Q capital framework, CET1/Tier 1/Tier 2 capital components, and PCA provisions—but these are regulatory definitions, not market catalysts. Excluded from archive scope.
